name: How much does window installation typically cost in my area?
headline: How much does window installation typically cost in my area?

mainEntity.name: How much does window installation typically cost in Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, Hillsborough, or Half Moon Bay?
mainEntity.text: How much does window installation typically cost in Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, Hillsborough, or Half Moon Bay?

acceptedAnswer.text: For most homes in Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, and nearby areas, you can expect to pay roughly $500 to $1,000 per window installed, depending on window type and complexity. That range usually includes labor and a mid-range, energy-efficient window. Larger homes or houses with many custom sizes might see total projects run from about $5,000 up to $15,000 or more. Several factors change the price. Vinyl windows are usually the most affordable, while wood, fiberglass, or specialty finishes cost more. Bay, bow, and picture windows are more expensive than standard double-hung or sliding windows. Second-story installations, stucco exteriors, and hard-to-access openings can increase labor costs. Watch out for common pitfalls when budgeting. Don’t forget extras like trim repairs, painting, disposal of old windows, and possible stucco or siding patching. A very low estimate may leave out important details like waterproof flashing or interior finish work, which you’ll pay for later. Be cautious with "per window" prices that don’t explain what’s included. Ask for a written breakdown of materials, labor, and any potential change orders. And leave a cushion of 10–20% in your budget for surprises such as hidden rot or framing repairs discovered once the old windows come out.

description: For most homes in Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, and nearby areas, you can expect to pay roughly $500 to $1,000 per window installed, depending on window type
